cadence: one X and one LinkedIn candidate per active business per day

business.name: Agentic Evidence
business.primary_job: Agentic technical interview evidence layer for Hatchways-style assessments.
business.current_motion: Candidate AI-evidence entry, reviewer handoff, repo-packaged Claude JSONL exports, assignment packet, guided Claude VM behavior, and finish checklist are live; 2026-05-04 commit `4f37230` added `/candidate/:sessionId/task`, `ae-task`, `ae-submit-steps`, Claude guidance injection, and procedural-prompt score filtering.
business.next_move: Add hidden-test grading, optional GitHub App install UX, and reviewer analysis fields over captured sessions.

x.copy:
Agentic Evidence records how candidates use Codex or Claude inside a provisioned assessment VM.

The useful proof is not a score alone. It is the transcript, git diff, terminal evidence, and reviewer packet.

https://agentic-evidence.fly.dev

linkedin.copy:
Agentic Evidence is the assessment layer I would want if candidates are allowed to use Codex or Claude.

The product records the AI session, terminal output, git snapshot, hidden-test result, and reviewer packet inside a provisioned developer VM.

The next useful move is a tighter GitHub App install path for assessment platforms.

https://agentic-evidence.fly.dev

business.name: Bring Your AI
business.primary_job: Local-first Claude Code to Codex harness migration.
business.current_motion: Product, payments, agent-readable commerce, direct Stripe checkout, hold-slot fallback, and `/pricing` page are live after design pass commit `30508b5`; `foundry-businesses/portable` is only a marketing mirror on 1P.
business.next_move: Continue Claude/Codex-user distribution; SPT/ACP and x402/MPP remain private-preview-gated.

x.copy:
Bring Your AI is for the awkward handoff from Claude Code to Codex.

It keeps the migration local, maps instruction files and MCP config, and leaves validation notes where behavior does not port cleanly.

https://bringyour.ai

linkedin.copy:
Bring Your AI is the local-first path for moving a Claude Code setup into Codex.

The useful part is not copying one instruction file. It audits rules, skills, MCP config, hooks, and non-equivalent behavior so the migration leaves a validation trail.

The live routes are agent-readable and checkout-ready.

https://bringyour.ai

business.name: AI Dev Board
business.primary_job: AI/ML jobs board with REST API, MCP server, company claims, and promoted listings.
business.current_motion: API/MCP/indexed-job distribution plus employer conversion flow; `/post` plan-first flow and `/jobs` canonical redirect are live after design pass commit `ddc8b65`; `/tag/agents` visual cleanup is live in commit `4c30bea` with `AI Agent Jobs` title/meta and compact hero/search/filter layout; `foundry-businesses/ai-dev-jobs` is only a marketing/outreach mirror on 1P.
business.next_move: Keep API invariant green; improve SEO indexing and hiring-side conversion surfaces.

x.copy:
AI Dev Board is not just a job board page.

It exposes AI/ML roles through REST, MCP, RSS, and agent-readable commerce routes so agents can search and employers can convert without screen-scraping.

https://aidevboard.com

linkedin.copy:
AI Dev Board is a useful distribution surface because the API is the product, not an afterthought.

The board exposes AI/ML jobs through REST, MCP, RSS, and indexed pages. The current constraint is keeping that agent path green while improving employer conversion.

https://aidevboard.com

x.copy:
Not Human Search scores whether a site is usable by agents.

The useful marketing surface is the same as the product surface: searchable scores, MCP tools, OpenAPI, and proof routes agents can call.

https://nothumansearch.ai

linkedin.copy:
Not Human Search is the portfolio’s agent-readiness test bed.

It scores sites on concrete surfaces such as llms.txt, OpenAPI, structured APIs, MCP, and agent manifests. The next constraint is more high-fit directory distribution and better agent-first coverage.

https://nothumansearch.ai
